---
title: "Augmented generation"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 50%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Augmented generation {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

How to add knowledge to LLMs and let them act on it. We will build a simple RAG system, which leads naturally into tool calling.

# Manual RAG {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Context in, better answers out (10 min)

-   "Retrieval-Augmented Generation" without any fancy infrastructure
-   Stuff the relevant docs into the prompt yourself
-   Most "RAG" is this idea plus automation

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `13-coding-assistant` and try a coding assistant on a niche package (R: `weathR`, Python: `NWS`):

-   First, ask with no context and note what goes wrong.
-   Then paste the README into the prompt and compare.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

# RAG {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## How RAG actually works (30 min)

-   Chunk your documents
-   Compute embeddings for each chunk
-   At query time, find the most similar chunks and add them to the prompt
-   The "retrieval" is just nearest-neighbor search

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `14-rag` and build a dynamic RAG system:

-   Source documents: R for Data Science (R) or Polars Cookbook (Python).
-   Tools: `ragnar` (R), `llama-index` or chatlas helpers (Python).
-   Wire retrieval up as a tool the model can call.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=20 >}}

# Tool calling {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Letting the model use your functions (20 min)

-   The model decides when to call; you decide what is callable
-   The shape: function name, description, JSON schema for arguments
-   You execute the function and send the result back

## Demos

-   Manual loop (`_demos/manual-tools`): see every step
-   Automatic loop (`_demos/tools`): let the library handle the round trips

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `15-quiz-game-tools` and wire tool calling into the quiz show app:

-   Provide a function that plays a sound (R: `beepr`, Python: `playsound`).
-   Document it well and register it as a tool in the quiz show app.
-   Watch the model learn when to play which sound.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=20 >}}

# Tool calling UI {background-image="../images/undraw_ai-research-assistant_cxx0.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Showing tool activity to users (30 min)

-   Tool annotations can give each tool an icon and title
-   `ContentToolResult` can return a custom title and icon
-   Tool calls can update reactive state in your Shiny app

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `16-quiz-game-ui` and progressively upgrade the quiz show app:

-   Add tool annotations: give each tool an icon and title.
-   Return a `ContentToolResult` with a custom title and icon.
-   Track answers and score in reactive value boxes that update from tool calls.
-   (Optional) Add a "finish round" tool that displays the final score.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=20 >}}


# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/)
:::
